english: turn one's back upon a thing; to throw away, throw down, a stone, a corpse etc.; to eject, lúd-pa (lud pa) phlegm; to throw off, a rider; to give up, abandon, a work; to forsake, a friend; to abort. - skyúr-ma (skyur ma) abortion W. (?) — ču skyúr (chu skyur), ryańskyúr (rgyang skyur) capital punishment in C., when the delinquent, with a weight fastened to his neck, is thrown from a rock into a river.
Sch.: skyus tóg-pa (skyus tog pa) altogether; skyús-su klóg-pa (skyus su klog pa) Gramm.: to pronounce jointly, viz two consonants without a vowel between them.
1 v. ske (ske). - 2. v. skyed (skyed) and skyé-ba (skye ba).

I. vb. (भव) pf. skyes (skyes) 1. to be born; ñá-la (nya la) (seld. las (las)) bu skyés-pa yín (bu skyes pa yin) I have given birth to a son Glr.; pó-skyes (pho skyes) a man, mó skyes (mo skyes) a woman, female; skye-rga-na-č́i-bai sdug-bsńál (skye rga na chi bai sdug bsngal) the evil of birth, old age, sickness and death (which constitute what in the opinion of the Buddhist is the greatest evil of all, that of existence); tóg-ma skyés-nas (thog ma skyes nas), má-la skyés-nas (ma la skyes nas) B., *á-ma skyé-sa-na* (a ma skye sa na) W. from one's birth; skye č́i- (skye chi-) (or ši- (shi-)) méd pa (med pa) subject neither to birth nor to death, eternal; skye-ₒgag-méd-Thgy. (skye 'gag med Thgy.), Lex., is said to mean the same. In the special sense of the doctrine of metempsychosis skyé-ba (skye ba) has often to be rendered by: to be re-born, mí-ru (mi ru) as man, bur (bur) as (somebody's) son. — mi skyé-bai čós-la bzód-pa (mi skye bai chos la bzod pa) v. bzód-pa (bzod pa). — W.: *skyé-če* (skye che) 1. as inf. to be born, reborn. 2. as sbst. the being born; birth. 3. as adj. being with child, pregnant; big with young, also *skyé-če-ma* (skye che ma). — 2. to become, to begin to exist, arise, nad kun mí skye (nad kun mi skye), skyés-paañ źi-bai p'yír (skyes pa'ang zhi bai phyir) ut ne morbus ullus nascatur, natus quoque sedetur Med.; skye-ba dań ₒjig-pa (skye ba dang 'jig pa) to arise and pass away; frq. of thoughts, passions etc. (the person as well as the thing in the accus.): k'yeu k'rós-pai sems skyés-te (khye'u khros pai sems skyes te) the youth — thoughts of wrath arising (in him). — 3. to grow (nasci) lúń-pa ₒbru skyé-ba (lung pa 'bru skye ba) valleys where corn grows; ru mgó-la skye (ru mgo la skye) a horn is growing on the head. — 4. to grow (crescere) čer (cher) or čen-por skyé-ba (chen por skye ba) to grow up, to grow tall; ras kyań lús-kyi tsád-du skyés-so (ras kyang lus kyi tshad du skyes so) the garment also grew in proportion to the growth of the body, or: with the body Dzl.; rtúl-pod-par skyés-so (rtul phod par skyes so) he grew up a valiant man, became a valiant man; to bud, germinate, sprout, *sbáńs-te skye čúg-ce* (sbangs te skye chug ce) to accelerate the germinating of the seed by maceration W.; even = p'él-ba (phel ba) Dzl. ? — 5. sometimes = skyá-ba (skya ba) 2. unless in that case *kyé-če* (skye che) should be spelled bskyás-čes (bskyas ches) W. II. sbst. (जाति) 1. the being born, the birth, skyé-ba mtó-ba (skye ba mtho ba), skye-mtó (skye mtho) or mtóń (mthong) high birth; of high birth, noble, man, male; skyé-ba dmá-ba (skye ba dma' ba), skye-dmá (skye dma'), -dmán (dman) low birth; of low birth, ignoble, woman, mí-lus t'ob kyań skyé-ba dman (mi lus thob kyang skye ba dman) born a human being, it is true, but only a female Mil.; skyes-dmán (skyes dman) col. *kyer mán* (kyer man) in C. the usual word for woman and wife, ñe kyer mén (nye kyer men) my wife. — In the special Buddhistic sense: re-birth mir skyé-ba bźén-pa (mir skye ba bzhen pa) to take or assume re-birth as a human being; also period of re-birth = existence, life, skyé-ba ₒdi-la (skye ba 'di la) in this, my present, period of life; skyé-ba bdun (skye ba bdun) seven periods of life; also manner of re-birth, v. skye-gnás (skye gnas); in a concrete sense: the re-born individual, yúm-gyi skyé-ba yín (yum gyi skye ba yin) she is the re-birth of the queen dowager, the re-born q. d. — 2. the arising etc. — 3. the growing etc. Comp. skye-dgú (skye dgu) v. skyé-bo (skye bo). — skye-ₒgró (skye 'gro) = ₒgroba ('gro ba) being (q.v.) — skye-sgó (skye sgo) 1. entrance to re-birth, viz. to one of the six regions of birth, v. ₒgró-ba ('gro ba) II., skye-sgó gčód-pa (skye sgo gcod pa) to lock it up. 2. face, légs-pa (legs pa) a handsome, ńan-pa (ngan pa) an ugly face; also k'a-sgó skye-bzo légs-pa (kha sgo skye bzo legs pa) is said for: having a handsome exterior C. — skye-mčéd (skye mched) (आयतन) the five (or six) seats, i. e. organs, of the senses (the sixth is मनस् the inner sense); the senses themselves; this conception, however, has been greatly altered and varied by the fanciful theories of medical and philosophical authors, cf. Burn. I, 500. Was. (240). — skye-gnás (skye gnas) 1. birthplace; station or locality of a plant.
